criteria.label: Explain why @deco is equivalent to fn = deco(fn) and when it runs.
criteria.example:
```python
@timing
def compute(): ...
# is identical to:
def compute(): ...
compute = timing(compute)
```
The decorator runs *once* at definition time; the wrapper runs on every call.

criteria.label: Write a decorator that forwards *args/**kwargs and preserves metadata with functools.wraps.
Implement a parametrized decorator (a function that returns a decorator) such as @retry(times=3).
Decide when lru_cache is safe and predict the effect of stacking multiple decorators.
criteria.example:
```python
@functools.lru_cache(maxsize=1024)   # outer: applied second
@timing                              # inner: applied first
def embed(text: str) -> tuple[float, ...]: ...
# call → cache check → (miss) → timing wrapper → embed → store. Decorators apply bottom-up.
```
lru_cache is safe only for *pure* functions with hashable args.
